article_title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute | TechCrunch

article_items.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute | TechCrunch
article_items.description: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.
article_items.excerpt: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.
article_items.summary: Under the terms of the deal, Google will pay SpaceX $920 million per month from October 2026 through June 2029 for access to “approximately 110,000 NVIDIA GPUs, CPUs, memory, and other related components.” The deal is similar in length and scope to the one SpaceX announced with Anthropic in late May. The company announced the deal in a regulatory filing on Friday . SpaceX announced the deal just one week before the company’s stock is expected to start trading on the Nasdaq exchange. CEO Elon Musk has previously suggested his company would reserve the Colossus 2 data center for xAI. The companies are also reportedly in talks to try to build orbital data centers - a major component of SpaceX’s future plans post-IPO. Anthropic was significantly limited in its compute capacity prior to its deal with SpaceX, raising usage limits on the same day the deal was announced.

key_points: As part of that deal, Anthropic agreed to pay SpaceX $1.25 billion per month through 2029 to rent all the available compute from its Colossus 1 data center near Memphis, Tennessee, that xAI - now part of SpaceX -.

The company announced the deal in a regulatory filing on Friday .
Anthropic was significantly limited in its compute capacity prior to its deal with SpaceX, raising usage limits on the same day the deal was announced.
SpaceX announced the deal just one week before the company’s stock is expected to start trading on the Nasdaq exchange.
Both SpaceX and Google have the option to terminate the agreement with 90 days’ notice after December 31, 2026.
Google’s deal appears to be paying for roughly half the amount of compute that Anthropic has access to at Colossus 1.
“Google Cloud and SpaceX are long-time partners,” Google said in a statement.
In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.
CEO Elon Musk has previously suggested his company would reserve the Colossus 2 data center for xAI.
To help with that, Alphabet recently announced an $80 billion equity sale.

article_items.description: A Blog post by Build Small Hackathon on Hugging Face
article_items.excerpt: A Blog post by Build Small Hackathon on Hugging Face
article_items.summary: A Blog post by Build Small Hackathon on Hugging Face Thousand Token Wood: shipping a multi-agent economy on a 3B model I built Thousand Token Wood for the Build Small Hackathon. The 1929 bank runs become the Run on Oona's Hoard. The South Sea Bubble becomes the Hollow Log Trading Company. I reframed it as a mean-reverting mood that recovers when a creature is fed and warm and never hits zero. Oona began liquidating her honey to raise pebbles, and the flood of supply crashed the honey price from 10 to 3 over the next turns. A Blog post by Build Small Hackathon on Hugging Face Thousand Token Wood: shipping a multi-agent economy on a 3B model I built Thousand Token Wood for the Build Small Hackathon.
They were frozen because the agents quoted back the reference price I showed them.
Each legend fires real shocks, and the agents react.
The fix was not a bigger model, it was a sharper prompt.
In one run I drew the Run on Oona's Hoard, the rumor that the owl's vault was empty.
It is a tiny economy: five woodland creatures, each its own agent on Qwen2.5-3B , trade five goods for pebbles, gossip, hoard, and panic.
The 3B emitted valid JSON on 100% of calls, but its economic judgment was poor: a creature that produced acorns would post an order to buy acorns, the one thing it had in surplus.
The market cleared once and went silent.

article_items.title: Trump to meet AI leaders over US investment in their companies
article_items.description: The US president said on Friday he expects to meet the leaders of top AI companies next week.
article_items.excerpt: The US president said on Friday he expects to meet the leaders of top AI companies next week.
article_items.summary: Trump compared the prospective investment in AI to the US government last year taking a 10% stake in Intel , a company that makes computer chips. Trump to meet AI leaders to discuss US investment in their companies US President Donald Trump is planning to meet the bosses of some of the country's most notable artificial intelligence (AI) companies to discuss the government taking a financial stake in their future. "We're talking about it,"Trump said, referring to conversations with AI leaders "where the American people can benefit from the success of AI, the American people will like it better". Part of the US investing directing in AI companies, however, would be to improve Americans' views of the technology, which have grown increasingly negative . The US president said on Friday he expects to meet the leaders of top AI companies next week.
Sanders recently said he intended to propose a sort of sovereign wealth fund in which the US would take a 50% stake in AI companies.
the latter two of which are expected to go public in the coming weeks .
Although the president did not name specific companies, the biggest companies in the US working on AI are Google, Microsoft, OpenAI, SpaceX and Anthropic.
Speaking on Air Force One, Trump said the goal of the US government investing in AI companies was to "create almost a partnership with the American public".
He expects to meet leaders of major AI companies at the White House.
"We're talking about it,"Trump said, referring to conversations with AI leaders "where the American people can benefit from the success of AI, the American people will like it better".
Part of the US investing directing in AI companies, however, would be to improve Americans' views of the technology, which have grown increasingly negative .
Asked about Sanders' plan, President Trump insisted he had been considering the US investing in AI companies for a year, but did not dismiss the senator's notion.

article_items.title: Google experiments with sending Chrome searches straight to AI - Engadget
article_items.description: Google seems to be testing a new feature that will take you to AI Mode by default when you do a search in Chrome.
article_items.excerpt: Google seems to be testing a new feature that will take you to AI Mode by default when you do a search in Chrome.
article_items.summary: At I/O 2026, it launched the new " Intelligent Search Box ," which can take videos, images, files and even Chrome tabs as inputs for search queries. But when the flag is enabled in Canary, you're taken straight to AI Mode, which looks and acts more like a chatbot conversation than your typical Google search results page. While Google says this isn't its intention, the company has been putting more and more AI features into its products recently.
